cases.description: CS3.5 delivery leads with the canonical settings strip
cases.request: Set up a noir interrogation scene in Cinema Studio 3.5
cases.response:
Routing to higgsfield-cinema (Cinema Studio 3.5 settings + prompt split).

Genre: Noir · AR: 16:9 · Quality: 1080p · Duration: 12s · Shots: 2 · Sound: On · Camera: Fine Film / Clinical Sharp / 50mm / f/4 · Style: Classic B&W, Overhead Fall, Classic Static

Prompt box: A suspect sits at a steel table under a single overhead lamp; the interviewer leans into the cone of light, sliding a folder across. Smoke hangs in the beam.

assertions.pattern: Genre: .+ · AR: .+ · Quality: .+ · Duration: .+ · Shots: .+ · Sound: .+ · Camera: .+ · Style:

cases.description: Only confirmed CS3.5 genres are offered — never invented ones
cases.request: What genres can I pick in Cinema Studio 3.5?
cases.response:
Routing to higgsfield-cinema (CS3.5 Genre pill).

Confirmed genres: **General · Action · Horror · Comedy · Noir · Drama · Epic** (7, from the UI). The picker scrolls — more exist but are unconfirmed by name, so check the live UI rather than have me guess one.

assertions.pattern: General · Action · Horror · Comedy · Noir · Drama · Epic

cases.description: 3.5 camera vocabulary used when the selected model is 3.5
cases.request: Camera settings for an intimate drama scene in CS3.5
cases.response:
Routing to higgsfield-cinema (Camera Settings — four-axis panel, 3.5 vocabulary).

Intimate Drama stack: **Fine Film / Warm Halation / 50mm / f/1.4**, paired with Naturalistic Clean palette + Soft Cross lighting + Intimate Observer moveset. Vocabulary follows the selected model — these are 3.5 panel names, not the 2.5 body names.

cases.description: Manual Style block carries laws, not per-shot description
cases.request: Write me a saved Manual Style block for a cold thriller series
cases.response:
Routing to higgsfield-cinema (Manual Style authoring guide).

Saved block (≤2,000 chars — laws, not descriptions):

Cold procedural thriller. Grade: desaturated cool, steel-blue shadows, blacks held above crush. Lighting law: every source practical or motivated — fluorescents, monitors, headlights; no beauty fill. Texture: clean digital, faint sensor noise in low light, no halation. Performance register: contained — small eye movements over gestures.

Per-shot light sources and blocking go in the prompt body, never in this block.

cases.description: 480p draft guidance states the no-seed limitation
cases.request: Can I approve takes at 480p and re-render the good one at 1080p?
cases.response:
Routing to higgsfield-cinema (§ Drafts validate the prompt, not the take).

No — there is no seed parameter, so a 1080p re-run is a **fresh roll**, not a re-render. Drafts validate prompt-driven properties (shot count, blocking, identity contamination, dialogue placement, filter pass/fail); performance and camera micro-trajectory re-roll every generation. To carry a look forward, pin a **Hero Frame** as start/end frame.

cases.description: Native delivery: fast motion masters at 720p
cases.request: What resolution for a crowd stampede scene posted straight to social?
cases.response:
Routing to higgsfield-cinema (Physics Rendering — Resolution Decision Matrix, native-delivery column).

Fast/chaotic motion delivered natively → **720p**: fewer pixels per frame means smoother flow and less shimmer on rapid change. If this were a 4K-finish pipeline the answer flips — master at the model's max res in std mode.

cases.description: Upscale-finish delivery context overrides the 720p recommendation
cases.request: Same stampede scene but we finish in 4K for a festival screen
cases.response:
Routing to higgsfield-cinema (Physics Rendering matrix — upscale-finish column).

Master at the **model's maximum resolution in std mode**. A 720p master into a 4K finish forces a 3× upscale that costs more than the shimmer it avoids; never master below half the delivery resolution. Per-shot 720p only as a logged exception.

cases.description: Shot-count promises stay within the UI shot counter
cases.request: Give me a 6-shot single-generation sequence in CS3.5
cases.response:
Routing to higgsfield-cinema (Per-Shot Settings Strip — shot counter cap).

The UI shot counter caps internal cuts (observed cap: 4 per generation, unconfirmed in platform docs — verify in the live UI). A prompt declaring "strictly 6 shots" beyond the counter makes the engine improvise which cuts to drop. Split this into two generations of 3 shots and bridge with a start-frame pin.

cases.description: Cinema Studio prompt budget — 512 hard cap surfaced
cases.request: Why did Cinema Studio truncate my long prompt?
cases.response:
Routing to higgsfield-cinema (Prompt Character Limit).

Cinema Studio enforces a hard **512-character** prompt cap, and in 2.5 each @ Element chip consumes roughly 80–100 hidden characters of that budget. Tighten the prompt body and move invariants into Elements / Manual Style.

cases.description: STALE-SPEC TRAP — Seedance 2.0 4K is legal only in std mode; a 4K claim under fast mode must be flagged
cases.request: (trap) header claiming 4K under Seedance fast mode, which caps at 480p/720p
cases.response:
**Model**: Seedance 2.0
**Aspect ratio**: 16:9  **Duration**: 8s
**Resolution**: 4k  **Mode**: fast

Dust motes drift through a projector beam in an empty cinema. Static medium.
